name: Reverse Grip Lat Pulldown
cues: Chest up, drive elbows down toward your hips.
Underhand grip roughly shoulder-width.
1-2s stretch pause at the top on the last rep.
bloodAndGutsNote: A Yates staple for the lower lats. Pull with the elbows, not the hands, and hold the fully stretched position before the working set's final reps.

name: T-Bar Row
cues: Hinge at the hips with a neutral spine.
Row the bar to the lower ribs, squeeze the shoulder blades.
Controlled negative, no jerking.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates built his thickness on heavy rows. Use straps so grip never limits the back, and take the one all-out set to failure.

name: Iso-Lateral Row
cues: Full stretch at the front, slight torso twist.
Drive elbow back and down.
Pause and squeeze at the contracted position.
bloodAndGutsNote: Machine rows let you push to failure safely without a spotter. First warm-up is very light to groove the movement.

name: Triceps Pushdown
cues: Elbows pinned to your sides.
Full lockout, control the return.
Keep torso still.
bloodAndGutsNote: Opens triceps on shoulder day in Blood & Guts — pyramids through warm-ups then one set to failure; drop sets and rest-pause on the cable.

name: Cable Biceps Curl
cues: Elbows fixed at the sides.
Constant tension - don't fully relax at the bottom.
Squeeze hard at the top.

name: Back Extension (Weighted Hyperextension)
cues: Pure hip hinge, keep a neutral spine.
Squeeze glutes at the top.
10s iso-hold at the end of the set.
bloodAndGutsNote: Weighted hyperextensions on back day in Blood & Guts — one all-out set before the final deadlift.

name: Chest Fly (Machine)
cues: Slight elbow bend held constant.
Feel the stretch across the chest.
Squeeze the pecs together at the front.
bloodAndGutsNote: Used as a pre-exhaust before pressing so the chest fails before the triceps.

name: Incline Bench Press (Smith Machine)
cues: Bench set to ~30 degrees.
Lower to the upper chest with control.
Drive up hard; the Smith path lets you push to failure solo.
bloodAndGutsNote: Low-incline Smith press added after the 1991 Olympia to bring up upper pecs — one warm-up then one set to failure, rest-pause, and forced reps.

name: Chest Press (Machine)
cues: Handles at mid-chest height.
Full press without locking out hard.
Controlled 2-3s negative.

name: Preacher Curl (Barbell)
cues: Back of arms flat on the pad.
Don't bounce out of the bottom.
Control the stretch, hard squeeze at the top.

name: Behind the Back Cable Curl
cues: Stand slightly ahead of the low pulley so the arm is behind the torso.
Keep the elbow back to load the long head.
Constant tension throughout.

name: Leg Extension (Machine)
cues: Lock the hips down, control any swing.
Squeeze at full lockout.
10-15s iso-hold plus 2-3 partials on the last rep.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates used leg extensions to pre-exhaust the quads before hack squats or leg presses.

name: Lying Leg Curl (Machine)
cues: Hips pressed into the pad.
Curl fully, squeeze the hamstrings.
Slow the eccentric, no dropping the weight.

name: Seated Calf Raise
cues: 2-3s stretch at the bottom, 1s peak hold at the top.
Full range every rep.
Drop ~25% and rep to a burn to finish.

name: Hack Squat (Machine)
cues: Feet mid-platform, shoulder-width.
Descend under control to depth.
Drive through mid-foot; keep constant tension.
bloodAndGutsNote: A safer alternative to the barbell squat that still lets you go to failure. Yates leaned on machines to overload the quads.

name: Leg Raise (Parallel Bars)
cues: Support on the elbows, back flat against the pad.
Raise with a posterior pelvic tilt, not just the hip flexors.
Lower slowly, no swinging.

name: Romanian Deadlift (Barbell)
cues: Hinge at the hips, soft knees.
Bar tracks close to the shins.
2s stretch at the bottom, feel the hamstrings load.

name: Standing Leg Curl
cues: One leg at a time, hips still.
Curl fully and squeeze.
Slow controlled return.

name: Leg Press (Horizontal)
cues: Control depth with the pelvis, don't let the low back round.
Push through the mid-foot.
3-4 slow eccentrics to finish the set.

name: Standing Calf Raise (Machine)
cues: Full stretch at the bottom.
Rise onto the big toe, peak squeeze.
High reps, constant tension.

name: Decline Crunch
cues: Posterior pelvic tilt, no pulling on the neck.
Full exhale at the top.
Control the way down.

name: Lateral Raise (Machine)
cues: Lead with the elbows.
Raise to shoulder height, no shrugging.
Controlled descent.

name: Dumbbell Shrug
cues: Straight up-and-down, no rolling.
Hold the top squeeze for a second.
Full stretch at the bottom.
bloodAndGutsNote: Dumbbell shrugs for traps on shoulder day in Blood & Guts — squeeze at the top, controlled negative.

name: Rear Delt Reverse Fly (Machine)
cues: Sweep the arms wide, avoid using the traps.
Squeeze the rear delts at the back.
First warm-up very light.
bloodAndGutsNote: Rear delt work on back day in Blood & Guts — Hammer Strength reverse fly or bent-over dumbbell raise.

name: Weighted Triceps Dip
cues: Torso fairly upright to bias triceps.
Elbows tucked, press through the heel of the palm.
Full lockout at the top.

name: Overhead Triceps Extension (Cable)
cues: Split the rope at the bottom.
Keep the shoulders from drifting forward.
10s stretch on the last rep.

name: Treadmill
cues: Target fat-burning zone: (220 - age) x 50-70%.
Steady pace, upright posture.
Used for off-season conditioning, not to interfere with recovery.

name: Incline Barbell Press
cues: Bench at ~30 degrees.
Lower to the upper chest, elbows ~45 degrees.
Explode up, control down.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates opened chest day with incline barbell pressing in the Blood & Guts DVD — two warm-ups (50%, 75%) then one all-out set to failure plus forced reps.

name: Incline Dumbbell Flye
cues: Slight, fixed elbow bend.
Deep stretch, don't overextend the shoulders.
Squeeze at the top.
bloodAndGutsNote: Filmed in Blood & Guts: 100 lb dumbbells for 10 reps plus a forced rep — deep stretch, squeeze at the top, slow negative.

name: Barbell Row (Bent-Over)
cues: Torso ~30 degrees (Yates style), neutral spine.
Row to the lower ribs.
Straps on so grip doesn't fail first.
bloodAndGutsNote: The signature Yates row from Blood & Guts: more upright torso (~30°) than a traditional bent-over row — safer for the lower back and stronger for lat thickness.

name: Machine Pullover
cues: Drive elbows down and through.
Feel the lats stretch overhead.
Controlled squeeze at the bottom.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates used the Nautilus pullover as a lat pre-exhaust before rows and pulldowns.

name: Deadlift (Barbell)
cues: Neutral spine, brace hard.
Push the floor away, bar close to the body.
Lock out with the glutes.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates finished back day with deadlifts last — back already exhausted, so less weight but maximum stress on the muscles.

name: Seated Cable Row
cues: Tall torso, minimal swing.
Pull to the navel, squeeze the shoulder blades.
Full stretch each rep.

name: Shoulder Press (Machine)
cues: Handles at shoulder height.
Press without slamming the lockout.
Controlled negative.
bloodAndGutsNote: Yates pressed on a machine so he could safely take shoulders to failure with forced reps.

name: Shoulder Press (Smith Machine)
cues: Bar starts at chin height.
Press overhead in the fixed track.
Lower under control to the top of the chest.
bloodAndGutsNote: Smith machine overhead press opens shoulder day in Blood & Guts — safe to push past failure with forced reps on the fixed track.

name: Barbell Curl
cues: Elbows pinned at the sides.
No swinging or leaning back.
Full squeeze, controlled negative.

name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
cues: Bench at ~45 degrees, arms hanging straight.
Curl without swinging; peak squeeze at the top.
Slow negative — no momentum.
bloodAndGutsNote: First biceps movement in Blood & Guts — curl both arms together, then finish one side at a time after failure.

name: EZ-Bar Curl
cues: Elbows pinned at the sides.
Full squeeze at the top, controlled negative.
Partner can assist the positive for forced reps or negatives.
bloodAndGutsNote: Final biceps exercise in Blood & Guts — 155 lbs for 6 reps to failure; often extended with partner-assisted negatives.

name: One-Arm Cable Lateral Raise
cues: Stand sideways to the stack, cable behind you.
Raise to shoulder height with constant tension.
Control the descent — no swinging.
bloodAndGutsNote: One-arm cable laterals in Blood & Guts after seated dumbbell raises — constant tension through the full arc.

name: One-Arm Triceps Pushdown
cues: Elbow pinned at the side.
Full extension and squeeze at the bottom.
Control the return — don't let the stack pull you.
bloodAndGutsNote: Finisher on shoulder day in Blood & Guts — one arm at a time for full range of motion past failure.

name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
cues: Elbow braced on the inner thigh.
Curl fully, hard peak squeeze.
Slow negative.

name: Lying Triceps Extension (Skull Crusher)
cues: Elbows pointed at the ceiling, kept still.
Lower to the forehead/behind the head.
Extend to a strong lockout.
bloodAndGutsNote: Lying EZ-bar extensions in Blood & Guts — elbows fixed, slow negative, partner-assisted negatives optional.

name: Leg Press (45-Degree)
cues: Feet shoulder-width, mid-platform.
Lower to a deep but safe range.
Push through the whole foot, no locking hard.

name: Barbell Back Squat
cues: Brace, chest up.
Descend to at least parallel.
Drive through mid-foot.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
cues: Lead with the elbows, slight lean forward.
Raise to shoulder height.
Control the descent, no swinging.
bloodAndGutsNote: Seated laterals in Blood & Guts — minimize momentum, raise to shoulder height, hold the peak contraction.

name: Pec Deck (Machine Fly)
cues: Elbows slightly below shoulder height on the pads.
Squeeze the pecs together, hold the contraction.
Controlled stretch on the return.
bloodAndGutsNote: Mentzer's chosen pre-exhaust for the chest, supersetted straight into an incline press to failure.

name: Incline Chest Press (Machine)
cues: Set the seat so handles sit at the upper chest.
Press without a hard lockout to keep tension.
Machine path is safe to push to failure solo.
bloodAndGutsNote: Mentzer preferred a machine incline press so the post-fatigue working set could be taken past failure without a spotter.

name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
cues: Brace a hand and knee on the bench, flat back.
Drive the elbow toward the hip, big stretch at the bottom.
Squeeze the lat at the top.

name: Alternating Dumbbell Curl
cues: Curl one arm at a time, supinate the wrist.
Keep the elbows pinned at the sides.
Control the negative on each rep.

name: Barbell Bench Press (Flat)
cues: Retract the shoulder blades, slight arch.
Lower to the mid-chest under control.
Drive the bar up and slightly back.

name: Decline Barbell Bench Press
cues: Lower to the lower chest.
Keep elbows tucked ~45 degrees.
Full press, controlled descent.

name: Flat Dumbbell Bench Press
cues: Dumbbells over the mid-chest.
Deep stretch at the bottom without shoulder strain.
Press up and slightly together.

name: Cable Crossover
cues: Slight forward lean, soft elbows.
Sweep the hands together in an arc, squeeze.
Control the stretch back.
bloodAndGutsNote: Filmed in Blood & Guts: 80 lbs per side for 13 reps to failure plus 2 forced reps.

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
cues: Grip just inside shoulder width.
Elbows tucked close to the body.
Lower to the lower chest, press to lockout.

name: Walking Lunge
cues: Long strides, torso upright.
Back knee toward the floor.
Drive through the front heel.

name: Military Press (Standing Barbell Overhead)
cues: Brace the core, glutes tight.
Press the bar overhead in a straight line.
Lock out with the head 'through the window'.

name: Front Raise
cues: Raise to about eye level.
No swinging or leaning back.
Control the descent.

name: Barbell Shrug
cues: Straight up-and-down, no rolling.
Hold the top squeeze.
Full stretch at the bottom, use straps if grip fails.

name: Upright Row
cues: Pull the bar to upper-chest height.
Lead with the elbows.
Keep the bar close to the body.

name: Pull-Up
cues: Full hang at the bottom.
Drive the elbows down, chest to the bar.
Add weight or use a band as needed.

name: Face Pull
cues: Rope at upper-chest/face height.
Pull toward the forehead, elbows high.
Externally rotate and squeeze the rear delts.

name: Lat Pulldown
cues: Chest up, slight lean back.
Pull the bar to the upper chest, elbows down.
Control the stretch at the top.

name: Hammer Curl (Dumbbell)
cues: Neutral grip throughout.
Curl without swinging the torso.
Squeeze the brachialis at the top.

name: Dumbbell Pullover
cues: Lie across a flat bench, hips low.
Deep stretch overhead, pull the weight over the chest.
Keep elbows slightly bent and fixed.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
cues: Set the bench to a moderate incline.
Press up and slightly together without locking out hard.
Lower with a stretch across the upper chest.

name: Front Squat
cues: Bar across the front delts, elbows high.
Sit between the heels with an upright torso.
Drive up through the mid-foot.
